name: Primera Divisió
content:
The Primera Divisió is the top tier of professional football in Andorra and is organized by the Andorran Football Federation. It was founded in 1995 after the federation itself was established, replacing earlier informal amateur competitions that had existed since the 1970s. Since 1996, after Andorra joined UEFA and FIFA, its clubs have been eligible to participate in European competitions such as the UEFA Champions League and UEFA Conference League.

The league currently consists of 10 teams, which represent different parishes of Andorra. Each season typically runs from autumn to spring, and teams compete in a multi-round format where they play each other multiple times. After a regular phase, the league can split into groups (top and bottom halves) to determine the champion and relegation positions. The bottom teams face relegation to the Segona Divisió, while the best-performing clubs fight for the title.

The champion of the Primera Divisió earns qualification for the UEFA Champions League preliminary rounds, while other top finishers can qualify for the UEFA Conference League. All matches are played across federation-managed stadiums, as most clubs do not have their own dedicated grounds, with the exception of a few cases like FC Encamp. Despite being a small league, it plays an important role in developing football in Andorra and providing clubs with access to European competition.

name: Copa Constitució
content:
The Copa Constitució is the main national football cup competition in Andorra, organized by the Andorran Football Federation. It was established in 1990, with the first official edition played in 1991. The tournament runs every year as a knockout-style competition and includes clubs from different levels of Andorran football.

Since the mid-1990s, the competition has been affiliated with UEFA and FIFA, which means the winner earns a place in European competitions. Specifically, the cup champion qualifies for the UEFA Europa Conference League qualifying rounds, giving Andorran clubs a chance to compete internationally.

The Copa Constitució is considered an important domestic trophy alongside the league system. It is usually played between January and May and includes teams from both the Primera Divisió and lower divisions. Over the years, clubs like FC Santa Coloma and Inter Club d’Escaldes have been among the most successful teams in the competition.
